name: Butterfinger Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Mix all the dry ingredients in a large bowl.
3. In another bowl, cream together the butter, peanut butter, and sugar until smooth.
4. Beat in the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
5. Gradually add the dry mixture to the wet mixture, stirring until combined.
6. Pour the batter into two 9x13 inch baking pans or one cookie sheet lined with parchment paper.
7. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until set and lightly golden around the edges.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool completely in the pans.
9. While the bars are cooling, melt 3 cups of chocolate chips in a microwave-safe bowl, stirring occasionally, until smooth. Spread the melted chocolate over the cooled bars for frosting, or stir in 2/3 cup peanut butter to make a peanut butter spread instead.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
